en_title: The Past Cradles for Us an Unending Blessed Fulfillment
en_body:
Như-Nguyện sobbed upon my shoulder. I gathered her into my heart, one hand smoothing her long hair fallen loose and wet with tears, the other painfully caressing the back that never ceased convulsing beneath her anguished sobs. Upon the cabinet newly made into Thảo's altar, the face of the young man who had just forever departed from his young wife and two small children shimmered in candlelight and in the smoke of incense sticks curving into ash! Bright eyes, a joyous gaze as though encouraging whoever stood before him, a smile open and generous... Thảo had departed amid the grieving remembrance of so many! My heart suddenly ached, thinking of little Trung and little Như-Tiên sleeping deeply after fits of crying that pierced the hearts of the family around them through all the busyness of the funeral days. I hoped Như-Nguyện too would grow weary, and her body would let her forget for a while the suffering that tormented her beyond endurance! Yet night also would pass: I imagined the evenings when Như-Nguyện must lie alone upon the bed whose pillows and blankets once were warm with passion, now immense and empty of the familiar body—from its scent to its sleeping form... I shuddered before an idea too terrible even for the endurance of one standing outside!
 I held Như-Nguyện tightly to my heart, softly lamenting to myself,
—Such suffering! After crying, your eyes will ache and trouble you even more! Như-Nguyện, try to eat a little something, please!
—I suffer so much, Elder-Sister! Thảo! Why did You abandon me and go, Thảo! Whom am I to blame, Elder-Sister Vi-Nhân? I believe in no invisible Being—then whom can I resent, or beg to shelter me in this moment of despair..!!
—Let Elder-Sister go into the kitchen and find Như-Nguyện something to eat, yes! For several days Như-Nguyện has desired neither food nor drink! You must preserve your health so you may care for little Trung and little Như-Tiên! Như-Nguyện, try to rest against this pillow, yes, and I will warm a little light food for my darling!
 I helped Như-Nguyện sit against the pillow at the back of the sofa. Như-Nguyện bent down, sobbing. Her tears seemed never to have ceased through the whole week since Thảo's automobile accident, when he died at once after being thrown from the car and breaking his neck. I went into the kitchen and warmed a little crab-and-bamboo-shoot soup and some fried rice. Ladling them into a porcelain bowl and a small bowl, I carried them to the living-room table, raised Như-Nguyện, and fed her one small spoonful at a time, coaxing her to try to eat. Như-Nguyện could only tremble with sobs between spoonfuls of rice and soup; her teardrops fell into the soup bowl and rice bowl, grieving my heart!
 ***@***
 Như-Nguyện leaned upon the pillow propped against the headboard. She looked at me, her voice low and even...
—Can you imagine, Thảo has only just departed, yet most people, even those most intimate to me, all counsel me that: Time will make me forget, and the suffering of loss will end; that every loss, however immense and heavy, will find Time a miracle medicine! They do not understand that I do not want to forget Thảo! That when they speak so to me, I only fear my remembrances of Thảo and the days we shared will fade within my mind! To me, if I cannot remember Thảo, I betray the self I was in the days with Thảo! Whenever I watch our old family films, the images upon the television screen are not real, not near as the emotions through which I relive those moments of happiness! What shall become of me when those emotions are no longer clear as now? I am very afraid of Time. Because the demands of the busy and clamorous life around me will numb my mind with them; and gradually I shall no longer be myself, no longer belong to Thảo as before! I thank you for agreeing to stay with me during these days and months! I could never endure if you were not here with me!
—Elder-Sister understands all you say! I admire your Courage! You dare confront and wholly experience the emotions of suffering before your immeasurable loss! You dare live true to yourself and Thảo! Do you know that this is a Salvation in itself? And more than this, you remember Thảo on behalf of little Trung and little Như-Tiên! The two children are too young; their memories will not be deep enough to carry whole their feelings for their father! Through you and your love for Thảo, Thảo will remain forever a meaningful Presence within the three of you—mother and children! I remember when the Father of Thảo, Hiền, and me had newly died: only after we completed Father's 100-Day Vegetarian Rite did I truly feel how deeply the Emptiness of Father's absence from my life had engraved itself upon my consciousness. From then, it seemed I began growing accustomed to a Silence within my inner life! Because when Father died, I possessed no religious concept, nor any concept of the soul, I understood Father's death only as the Silence and Emptiness of his familiar form from my life. During those days, Mother and Thảo and Hiền also lived dimly as shadows: Silence appeared within me from that time! Therefore, I do not want Như-Nguyện to become acquainted with it as I did long ago! In the United States, when a child passes through an experience of loss, the schools and churches usually provide assistance such as counseling or therapy. In Việt-Nam, people avoid mentioning the child's private sorrow, as though to show reverence for the sacredness of loss. The child may easily drown within immature endurance, without understanding the very depth and weight of the loss! That is an omission among our people! Yet Western society is too clamorous with talk therapy: always, Balance is difficult to create!
—I thank you! You are a wondrous Elder-Sister by marriage! I do not know whether I could endure the emptiness of this house without Thảo anymore, were you not beside me! Sometimes I think of your family circumstance, and I do not know what to feel, what to think! I am not absurd enough to imagine a choice between losing Thảo like this, or enduring divorce in marriage as you did; yet sometimes I ask myself, my loss and your loss—which circumstance bears the lighter suffering??
—Buddhism speaks of the suffering of “separation by death” and “separation in life.” And two sufferings: having to live beside one we hate, or far from one we love: both sufferings are nearly impossible to endure. Loss engraves wounds upon our consciousness! Yet the lucid understanding of the blessed vows once shared through sweetness and bitterness: memories that never cease being precious within the mind may soothe the wound! If there was anger, hatred: then Forgiveness heals bitterness! If there was happiness together: then Love nourishes the heart! I thank Như-Nguyện because I know the love Như-Nguyện bears for Thảo will forever keep Thảo alive within you and little Trung and little Như-Tiên! And think this way: whatever may be, Thảo forever remains beautiful within Như-Nguyện! Thảo loves Như-Nguyện! Thảo's love for Như-Nguyện will live forever within the love Như-Nguyện forever reserves for Thảo, without one blemish! That is the source of Consolation which we, the ones remaining, may receive as our unending blessed Fulfillment!

 Như-Nguyện reached to extinguish the bedside lamp and settled her body comfortably. In the deepening darkness, the young widow's breathing was light and even,
—Goodnight, Elder-Sister! Thank you, listen, my beloved Elder-Sister! Nevertheless, having you here to speak of Thảo, to remember Thảo, is still a Happiness I possess! And, I hope tonight I shall dream of Thảo with me! That too is a Consolation I hope I may receive!

note: The Buddhist phrases “separation by death” and “separation in life” name two forms of suffering, while the 100-Day Vegetarian Rite is a Vietnamese memorial observance for the dead.
